studies.study_title: Effectiveness and implementation of a multi-faceted intervention to facilitate adoption of asthma self-management practices in Peruvian children and adolescents: a hybrid type 2 individually randomized controlled trial

primary_outcome.outcome: Asthma Control Test (ACT) score at 6 months
primary_outcome.between_group_difference: No statistically significant difference between intervention and control arms at 6 months
primary_outcome.note: Both arms showed improvement; null result between arms

components: Designated nurse manager providing monthly home visits and phone/text support
Contextually adapted personalized asthma action plan
Education program based on NHLBI "Respirar es vida / A Breath of Life" curriculum
Interactive learning materials (flipcharts, videos, discussion, monitoring and feedback)
Super Niño booklet (comic book format educational material)
Free inhaled corticosteroids delivered to home
intervention_type.control_condition: One-time basic asthma education visit + free inhaled corticosteroids

primary_outcome_measure.name: Childhood/Adolescent Asthma Control Test (cACT/aACT)
primary_outcome_measure.description: Asthma control score at 6 months of follow-up
primary_outcome_measure.scoring: Higher score indicates better asthma control

study_design.type: Hybrid Type 2 Effectiveness-Implementation Individually Randomized Controlled Trial
study_design.randomization: 1:1 allocation using block randomization with block sizes of 4, 6, and 8
study_design.setting: Pediatric pulmonary clinic at Cayetano Heredia Hospital, Lima, Peru
study_design.target_population: Children aged 5-17 years with physician-diagnosed asthma in urban Lima, Peru

studies.error: File corrupted - contains only 16 bytes with error text 'error code: 1020'
studies.note: Cloudflare/CDN access restriction during download. File needs to be re-downloaded from original source.

studies.study_title: Weakly Supervised Patch Annotation for Improved Screening of Diabetic Retinopathy

effect_size.description: Performance gain in AUPRC on DDR(-) dataset with Inception-NetV3

studies.confidence_interval: Not explicitly reported in the extracted text

intervention_type.description: SAFE (Similarity-based Annotation via Feature-space Ensemble) - a two-stage framework that unifies weak supervision, contrastive learning, and patch-wise embedding inference to systematically expand sparse annotations
components: Stage 1: Dual-arm Patch Embedding Network (PEN) learns semantically structured, class-discriminative embeddings
Stage 2: Ensemble of independent embedding spaces extrapolates labels to unannotated regions based on spatial and semantic proximity

primary_outcome_measure.name: Patch-level annotation accuracy for diabetic retinopathy screening

study_design.description: Weakly supervised learning framework evaluation with comparative analysis against baseline approaches
